**User:**

<user_message>
iOS device isn't seeing any other devices... plus messages aren't coming through - so that BLE fail + LAN/WiFi Fil + mesh fail + direct fail.

As I typed that, the messages came through - it took WAY too long, given all those options for transport. Make sure that the timing is perfect for messaging, so that the previously used/good path is tried again, and if the message takes longer than ~500ms then, initiate all available transports. When there are 3 transports available like this, we need to ensure messaging is super quick and reliable. Ensure that we have graceful message colission handling, to allow multiple transports to attempt simultaneously with no UI issues - successfully delivering the correct number of message(s) even if there are multiple revieved. the first of each unique message should initiate the response call for status recieved, then duplicate messages should be marked as duplicate, and notate the time variance in reciept to drive mesh enhancements.
</user_message>

 16 | /// Default settings for mesh service configuration

 23 | /// Repository abstracting access to the Rust core via UniFFI bindings.
 24 | ///
 25 | /// This is the single source of truth for:
 26 | /// - Mesh service lifecycle
 27 | /// - Contacts management
 28 | /// - Message history
 29 | /// - Connection ledger
 30 | /// - Network settings
 31 | /// - Relay enforcement (relay = messaging bidirectional control)
 32 | ///
 33 | /// All UniFFI objects are initialized lazily and managed here to ensure
 34 | /// proper lifecycle and resource cleanup.
 35 | @MainActor
 36 | @Observable
 37 | final class MeshRepository {
 38 |     private enum IdentityBackupStore {
 39 |         static let service = "com.scmessenger.identity"
 40 |         static let account = "identity_backup_v1"
 41 |     }
 42 |     private enum InstallMarker {
 43 |         static let key = "mesh_install_marker_v1"
 44 |     }
 45 |     private let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.scmessenger", category: "Repository")
 46 |     
 47 |     /// Conditional logging - only log verbose messages in DEBUG builds
 48 |     private func logVerbose(_ message: @autoclosure @escaping () -> String) {
 49 |         #if DEBUG
 50 |         logger.debug("\(message())")
 51 |         #endif
 52 |     }
 53 |     
 54 |     private func logDiagnostic(_ message: @autoclosure @escaping () -> String) {
 55 |         #if DEBUG
 56 |         appendDiagnostic(message())
 57 |         #endif
 58 |     }
 59 |     private let storagePath: String
 60 |     private let diagnosticsLogFileName = "mesh_diagnostics.log"
 61 |     private let diagnosticsIOQueue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.scmessenger.diagnostics.io", qos: .utility)
 62 |     private var diagnosticsBuffer: [String] = []
 63 |     private let diagnosticsMaxLines = 1000
 64 |     private var heartbeatTimer: Timer?
 65 | 
 66 |     // MARK: - Bootstrap Nodes for NAT Traversal
 67 | 
 68 |     /// Static fallback bootstrap node multiaddrs for NAT traversal and internet roaming.
 69 |     /// These are used only if env override and remote fetch both fail/are absent.
 70 |     /// Priority order: GCP relay (cloud) → OSX relay (home/local backup).
 71 |     private static let staticBootstrapNodes: [String] = [
 72 |         // AWS Relay (Stable backup)
 73 |         "/ip4/34.135.34.73/tcp/9001/p2p/12D3KooWETatHYo4xt9aufXEEDce719fyMEB7KmXJga1SYVUikaw",
 74 |     ]
 75 | 
 76 |     /// Resolved bootstrap nodes using the core BootstrapResolver.
 77 |     /// Priority: SC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES env var → remote URL → static fallback.
 78 |     static let defaultBootstrapNodes: [String] = {
 79 |         let config = BootstrapConfig(
 80 |             staticNodes: staticBootstrapNodes,
 81 |             remoteUrl: "https://bootstrap.scmessenger.net/nodes.json",
 82 |             fetchTimeoutSecs: 5,
 83 |             envOverrideKey: "SC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES"
 84 |         )
 85 |         return BootstrapResolver(config: config).resolve()
 86 |     }()
 87 | 
 88 |     private static func isEnabledFlag(_ raw: String?) -> Bool {
 89 |         guard let value = raw?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).lowercased() else {
 90 |             return false
 91 |         }
 92 |         return value == "1" || value == "true" || value == "yes" || value == "on"
 93 |     }
 94 | 
 95 |     private static let bootstrapRelayPeerIds: Set<String> = Set(
 96 |         defaultBootstrapNodes.compactMap { parseBootstrapRelay(from: $0)?.relayPeerId }
 97 |     )

  9 | import CoreBluetooth
 10 | import os
 11 | 
 12 | /// Advertises self and serves GATT characteristics (iOS Peripheral role)
 13 | ///
 14 | /// Responsibilities:
 15 | /// - BLE advertising with mesh service
 16 | /// - GATT server for Message/Sync/Identity characteristics (matches Android GATT)
 17 | /// - Handle central subscriptions
 18 | /// - Privacy rotation (15 min intervals)
 19 | final class BLEPeripheralManager: NSObject {
 20 |     private let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.scmessenger", category: "BLE-Peripheral")
 21 |     private var peripheralManager: CBPeripheralManager!
 22 |     private weak var meshRepository: MeshRepository?
 23 | 
 24 |     // GATT Service and Characteristics (names match Android BleGattServer)
 25 |     private var meshService: CBMutableService?
 26 |     private var messageCharacteristic: CBMutableCharacteristic?  // Write: central → peripheral
 27 |     private var syncCharacteristic: CBMutableCharacteristic?     // Notify: peripheral → central
 28 |     private var identityCharacteristic: CBMutableCharacteristic? // Read: identity beacon
 29 | 
 30 |     // Subscribed centrals
 31 |     private var subscribedCentrals: [CBCentral] = []
 32 | 
 33 |     // Privacy rotation
 34 |     private var rotationInterval: TimeInterval = MeshBLEConstants.privacyRotationInterval
 35 |     private var rotationTimer: Timer?
 36 |     private var identityData: Data?
 37 | 
 38 |     // Notification Buffer (Performance Optimization)
 39 |     private var pendingNotifications: [(central: CBCentral, data: Data)] = []
 40 | 
 41 |     // Advertising state
 42 |     private var isAdvertising = false
 43 |     private var isRotationEnabled = true
 44 | 
 45 |     // Reassembly buffers per central
 46 |     private var reassemblyBuffers: [UUID: [Int: Data]] = [:]
 47 |     private var expectedFragments: [UUID: Int] = [:]
 48 | 
 49 |     init(meshRepository: MeshRepository) {
 50 |         self.meshRepository = meshRepository
 51 |         super.init()
 52 |         peripheralManager = CBPeripheralManager(
 53 |             delegate: self,
 54 |             // Keep all mutable BLE state on main to avoid cross-queue races
 55 |             // with repository send paths (subscribedCentrals/pendingNotifications).
 56 |             queue: .main,
 57 |             options: [CBPeripheralManagerOptionRestoreIdentifierKey: MeshBLEConstants.peripheralRestoreId]
 58 |         )
 59 |     }
 60 | 
 61 |     // MARK: - Public API
 62 | 
 63 |     func startAdvertising() {
 64 |         logger.info("Starting BLE advertising")
 65 |         guard peripheralManager.state == .poweredOn else {
 66 |             logger.warning("Cannot start advertising: BLE not powered on")
 67 |             return
 68 |         }
 69 | 
 70 |         setupService()
 71 |         startPrivacyRotation()
 72 |     }
 73 | 
 74 |     func stopAdvertising() {
 75 |         logger.info("Stopping BLE advertising")
 76 |         peripheralManager.stopAdvertising()
 77 |         peripheralManager.removeAllServices()
 78 |         rotationTimer?.invalidate()
 79 |         rotationTimer = nil
 80 |         isAdvertising = false
 81 |     }
 82 | 
 83 |     func setIdentityData(_ data: Data) {
 84 |         // This payload is served over GATT identity characteristic reads, not
 85 |         // advertisement service data. Full JSON identity is expected here.
 86 |         identityData = data
 87 |         logger.debug("Identity data updated for dynamic read: \(data.count) bytes")
 88 |     }
 89 | 
 90 |     func setRotationInterval(_ interval: TimeInterval) {
 91 |         rotationInterval = interval
 92 |         logger.debug("Rotation interval set: \(interval)s")
 93 |         if isAdvertising && isRotationEnabled {
 94 |             rotationTimer?.invalidate()
 95 |             startPrivacyRotation()
 96 |         }
 97 |     }
 98 | 
 99 |     func setRotationEnabled(_ enabled: Bool) {
100 |         isRotationEnabled = enabled
101 |         logger.debug("Rotation enabled: \(enabled)")
102 |         if isAdvertising {
103 |             if enabled {
104 |                 startPrivacyRotation()
105 |             } else {
106 |                 rotationTimer?.invalidate()
107 |                 rotationTimer = nil
108 |             }
109 |         }
110 |     }
111 | 
112 |     func applyAdvertiseSettings(intervalMs: UInt32, txPowerDbm: Int8) {
113 |         logger.debug("Advertise settings: interval=\(intervalMs)ms, txPower=\(txPowerDbm)dBm")
114 |         // iOS doesn't allow direct control of advertising interval/power
115 |         // Settings are advisory only
116 |     }
117 | 
118 |     func broadcastDataToCentrals(_ data: Data) {
119 |         if !Thread.isMainThread {
120 |             DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
121 |                 self?.broadcastDataToCentrals(data)
122 |             }
123 |             return
124 |         }
125 |         for central in subscribedCentrals {
126 |             _ = sendDataToCentral(central, data: data)
127 |         }
128 |     }
129 | 
130 |     /// Send data to a subscribed central identified by its UUID string.
131 |     /// Used when we are acting as Peripheral and need to push data to a Central peer.
132 |     @discardableResult
133 |     func sendDataToConnectedCentral(peerId: String, data: Data) -> Bool {
134 |         if !Thread.isMainThread {
135 |             // Use async to avoid deadlock - result will be best-effort
136 |             DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
137 |                 self?.sendDataToConnectedCentral(peerId: peerId, data: data)
138 |             }
139 |             return true // Optimistic return for async path
140 |         }
141 |         guard let uuid = UUID(uuidString: peerId) else {
142 |             logger.warning("sendDataToConnectedCentral: invalid UUID string \(peerId)")
143 |             return false
144 |         }
145 |         guard let central = subscribedCentrals.first(where: { $0.identifier == uuid }) else {
146 |             logger.warning("sendDataToConnectedCentral: no subscribed central for \(peerId)")
147 |             return false
148 |         }
149 |         return sendDataToCentral(central, data: data)
150 |     }
151 | 
152 |     func subscribedCentralIds() -> [String] {
153 |         if !Thread.isMainThread {
154 |             // Return empty array for non-main-thread calls to avoid deadlock
155 |             // Callers should invoke this from main thread for accurate results
156 |             logger.warning("subscribedCentralIds called from background thread - returning empty")
157 |             return []
158 |         }
159 |         return subscribedCentrals.map { $0.identifier.uuidString }
160 |     }
161 | 
162 |     private func appendRepositoryDiagnostic(_ message: String) {
163 |         let meshRepository = self.meshRepository
164 |         Task { @MainActor in
165 |             // Diagnostics are best-effort and intentionally dispatched asynchronously so
166 |             // BLE delegate paths never synchronously touch MainActor-isolated repository state.
167 |             meshRepository?.appendDiagnostic(message)
168 |         }
169 |     }
170 | 
171 |     @discardableResult
172 |     private func sendDataToCentral(_ central: CBCentral, data: Data) -> Bool {
173 |         if !Thread.isMainThread {
174 |             // Use async to avoid deadlock - result will be best-effort
175 |             DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
176 |                 self?.sendDataToCentral(central, data: data)
177 |             }
178 |             return true // Optimistic return for async path
179 |         }
180 |         guard let messageCharacteristic else {
181 |             logger.warning("sendDataToCentral: message characteristic unavailable")
182 |             return false
183 |         }
184 |         guard subscribedCentrals.contains(where: { $0.identifier == central.identifier }) else {
185 |             logger.warning("sendDataToCentral: central no longer subscribed \(central.identifier)")
186 |             pendingNotifications.removeAll(where: { $0.central.identifier == central.identifier })
187 |             return false
188 |         }
189 |         let mtu = central.maximumUpdateValueLength
190 |         if mtu <= 4 {
191 |             logger.warning("sendDataToCentral: invalid central MTU \(mtu) for \(central.identifier)")
192 |             return false
193 |         }
194 |         let fragments = fragmentData(data, mtu: mtu)
195 |         var accepted = false
196 | 
197 |         for fragment in fragments {
198 |             if !subscribedCentrals.contains(where: { $0.identifier == central.identifier }) {
199 |                 logger.warning("sendDataToCentral: central unsubscribed mid-send \(central.identifier)")
200 |                 pendingNotifications.removeAll(where: { $0.central.identifier == central.identifier })

	  1 | //
  2 | //  MultipeerTransport.swift

  5 | //  WiFi Direct equivalent using Apple's Multipeer Connectivity
  6 | //  Transport priority: Multipeer > BLE > Internet

  8 | 
  9 | import Combine
 10 | import MultipeerConnectivity
 11 | import os
 12 | 
 13 | /// WiFi Direct equivalent using Apple's Multipeer Connectivity
 14 | ///
 15 | /// Multipeer Connectivity provides peer-to-peer WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity
 16 | /// without requiring internet infrastructure. Preferred over BLE for speed.
 17 | final class MultipeerTransport: NSObject {
 18 |     struct DiagnosticsSnapshot {
 19 |         let connectedPeers: Int
 20 |         let connectingPeers: Int
 21 |         let inviteInFlight: Int
 22 |         let inviteTimeoutCount: Int
 23 |         let inviteDeclineCount: Int
 24 |         let effectiveMediumEstimate: String
 25 |     }
 26 | 
 27 |     private let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.scmessenger", category: "Multipeer")
 28 |     private weak var meshRepository: MeshRepository?
 29 | 
 30 |     // Multipeer components
 31 |     private var peerID: MCPeerID!
 32 |     private var session: MCSession!
 33 |     private var advertiser: MCNearbyServiceAdvertiser?
 34 |     private var browser: MCNearbyServiceBrowser?
 35 | 
 36 |     // Service type (must be ≤15 chars, lowercase, no special chars)
 37 |     private let serviceType = "scmesh"
 38 | 
 39 |     // Connection state
 40 |     private var connectedPeers: Set<MCPeerID> = []
 41 |     private var connectingPeerNames: Set<String> = []
 42 |     private var isAdvertising = false
 43 |     private var isBrowsing = false
 44 | 
 45 |     // Reconnection state: maps peer display name → retry count
 46 |     private var reconnectAttempts: [String: Int] = [:]
 47 |     // Maximum reconnect attempts before giving up
 48 |     private let maxReconnectAttempts = 5
 49 |     // Base delay in seconds for exponential backoff
 50 |     private let reconnectBaseDelay: TimeInterval = 2.0
 51 |     // Invite storm guardrails for reconnect/discovery churn
 52 |     private var inviteInFlight: Set<String> = []
 53 |     private var lastInviteAttemptAt: [String: Date] = [:]
 54 |     private var inviteTimeoutWorkItems: [String: DispatchWorkItem] = [:]
 55 |     private var inviteTimeoutCount = 0
 56 |     private var inviteDeclineCount = 0
 57 |     private let inviteDebounceSeconds: TimeInterval = 4.0
 58 |     private let inviteTimeoutSeconds: TimeInterval = 12.0
 59 |     private let maxConcurrentInvites = 4
 60 | 
 61 |     init(meshRepository: MeshRepository) {
 62 |         self.meshRepository = meshRepository
 63 |         super.init()
 64 |         setupPeerID()
 65 |         setupSession()
 66 |     }
 67 | 
 68 |     private func identitySnippetForDisplayName() -> String {
 69 |         if Thread.isMainThread {
 70 |             return MainActor.assumeIsolated { meshRepository?.getIdentitySnippet() ?? "SCMesh" }
 71 |         }
 72 | 
 73 |         // Use async to avoid deadlock - return default name for background thread calls
 74 |         // The peer ID will be updated on next identity change via broadcastIdentityBeacon
 75 |         logger.warning("identitySnippetForDisplayName called from background thread - using default name")
 76 |         return "SCMesh"
 77 |     }
 78 | 
 79 |     private func appendRepositoryDiagnostic(_ message: String) {
 80 |         Task { @MainActor [weak meshRepository] in
 81 |             meshRepository?.appendDiagnostic(message)
 82 |         }
 83 |     }
 84 | 
 85 |     // MARK: - Setup
 86 | 
 87 |     private func setupPeerID() {
 88 |         // Use identity snippet as display name
 89 |         let displayName = identitySnippetForDisplayName()
 90 |         peerID = MCPeerID(displayName: displayName)
 91 |         logger.info("Multipeer peer ID: \(displayName)")
 92 |     }
 93 | 
 94 |     private func setupSession() {
 95 |         // .required encryption enforces TLS-like security for all Multipeer frames.
 96 |         // This is non-negotiable for a sovereign messenger.
 97 |         session = MCSession(
 98 |             peer: peerID,
 99 |             securityIdentity: nil,
100 |             encryptionPreference: .required
101 |         )
102 |         session.delegate = self
103 |     }
104 | 
105 |     // MARK: - Reconnection
106 | 
107 |     /// Schedule a reconnect attempt for a peer that dropped off.
108 |     ///
109 |     /// Uses exponential backoff: delay = base * 2^attempt, capped at 60 s.
110 |     /// Gives up after `maxReconnectAttempts` tries and removes the peer
111 |     /// from the retry table so it can be re-discovered organically.
112 |     private func scheduleReconnect(for peer: MCPeerID) {
113 |         let name = peer.displayName
114 |         let attempt = reconnectAttempts[name, default: 0]
115 | 
116 |         guard attempt < maxReconnectAttempts else {
117 |             logger.warning("Reconnect: giving up on \(name) after \(attempt) attempts")
118 |             reconnectAttempts.removeValue(forKey: name)
119 |             return
120 |         }
121 | 
122 |         let delay = reconnectBaseDelay * pow(2.0, Double(attempt))
123 |         let cappedDelay = min(delay, 60.0)
124 |         reconnectAttempts[name] = attempt + 1
125 | 
126 |         logger.info("Reconnect: scheduling attempt \(attempt + 1)/\(self.maxReconnectAttempts) for \(name) in \(Int(cappedDelay))s")
127 | 
128 |         DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + cappedDelay) { [weak self] in
129 |             guard let self else { return }
130 |             // Only invite if the peer is not already connected and we are still browsing
131 |             guard !self.connectedPeers.contains(peer), self.browser != nil else {
132 |                 self.logger.debug("Reconnect: skipping \(name) — already connected or not browsing")
133 |                 return
134 |             }
135 |             self.logger.info("Reconnect: re-inviting \(name)")
136 |             self.invitePeerIfAllowed(peer, source: "reconnect")
137 |         }
138 |     }
139 | 
140 |     private func clearInviteTracking(for peerName: String) {
141 |         inviteInFlight.remove(peerName)
142 |         inviteTimeoutWorkItems.removeValue(forKey: peerName)?.cancel()
143 |     }
144 | 
145 |     private func invitePeerIfAllowed(_ peer: MCPeerID, source: String) {
146 |         let name = peer.displayName
147 |         let now = Date()
148 | 
149 |         guard browser != nil else { return }
150 |         guard !connectedPeers.contains(peer) else {
151 |             appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_skipped id=\(name) source=\(source) reason=already_connected")
152 |             return
153 |         }
154 |         guard !connectingPeerNames.contains(name) else {
155 |             appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_skipped id=\(name) source=\(source) reason=already_connecting")
156 |             return
157 |         }
158 |         guard !inviteInFlight.contains(name) else {
159 |             appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_skipped id=\(name) source=\(source) reason=in_flight")
160 |             return
161 |         }
162 |         guard inviteInFlight.count < maxConcurrentInvites else {
163 |             appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_skipped id=\(name) source=\(source) reason=concurrency_limit in_flight=\(inviteInFlight.count)")
164 |             return
165 |         }
166 |         if let last = lastInviteAttemptAt[name], now.timeIntervalSince(last) < inviteDebounceSeconds {
167 |             appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_debounced id=\(name) source=\(source)")
168 |             return
169 |         }
170 | 
171 |         lastInviteAttemptAt[name] = now
172 |         inviteInFlight.insert(name)
173 |         browser?.invitePeer(peer, to: session, withContext: nil, timeout: 10)
174 |         appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_sent id=\(name) source=\(source) in_flight=\(inviteInFlight.count)")
175 | 
176 |         inviteTimeoutWorkItems[name]?.cancel()
177 |         let timeoutWork = DispatchWorkItem { [weak self] in
178 |             guard let self else { return }
179 |             guard self.inviteInFlight.contains(name) else { return }
180 |             self.inviteTimeoutCount += 1
181 |             self.clearInviteTracking(for: name)
182 |             self.appendRepositoryDiagnostic("multipeer_invite_timeout id=\(name) source=\(source) timeouts=\(self.inviteTimeoutCount)")
183 |         }
184 |         inviteTimeoutWorkItems[name] = timeoutWork
185 |         DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + inviteTimeoutSeconds, execute: timeoutWork)
186 |     }
187 | 
188 |     // MARK: - Public API
189 | 
190 |     func startAdvertising() {
191 |         guard !isAdvertising else {
192 |             logger.debug("Already advertising")
193 |             return
194 |         }
195 | 
196 |         advertiser = MCNearbyServiceAdvertiser(
197 |             peer: peerID,
198 |             discoveryInfo: nil,
199 |             serviceType: serviceType
200 |         )

271 |     private let connectedReemitInterval: TimeInterval = 15
272 | 
273 |     private func isTerminalIdentityFailure(_ errorCode: String?) -> Bool {
274 |         switch errorCode?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) {
275 |         case "identity_device_mismatch", "identity_abandoned":
276 |             return true
277 |         default:
278 |             return false
279 |         }
280 |     }
281 | 
282 |     private func terminalIdentityFailureMessage(_ errorCode: String?) -> String {
283 |         switch errorCode?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) {
284 |         case "identity_device_mismatch":
285 |             return "This contact's identity has been recycled onto another device. Refresh their contact details before retrying."
286 |         case "identity_abandoned":
287 |             return "This contact abandoned the identity you tried to reach. Re-verify the contact before sending again."
288 |         default:
289 |             return "This message was rejected because the recipient identity is no longer valid."
290 |         }
291 |     }
292 | 
293 |     // Reinstall detection: set true when Keychain has identity but disk has no contacts/history.
294 |     // Triggers aggressive post-start identity beacon to recover contacts from mesh peers.
295 |     private var isReinstallWithMissingData = false
296 | 
297 |     // P0: Dedup cache — suppress redundant peer-identified callbacks for the same peer
298 |     // within a 30-second window. The Rust core fires identify per-substream, producing
299 |     // 34K+ duplicate events in a typical session.
300 |     private var peerIdentifiedDedupCache: [String: (signature: String, observedAt: Date)] = [:]
301 |     private let peerIdentifiedDedupInterval: TimeInterval = 30
302 | 
303 |     // Transmission throttle to prevent rapid-click duplicates
304 |     private var transmissionThrottleCache: [String: Date] = [:]
305 |     private let transmissionThrottleInterval: TimeInterval = 1.0
306 | 
307 |     // P0: Throttle BLE identity beacon updates to 5s to reduce radio churn and bridge flood.
308 |     private var lastBleBeaconUpdate: Date?
309 |     private let bleBeaconUpdateInterval: TimeInterval = 5.0
310 |     private var lastBleBeaconPayload: Data?
311 |     private var lastBleBeaconPayloadPublishedAt: Date?
312 | 
313 |     // P1: Dedup cache — suppress duplicate disconnect callbacks for the same peer
314 |     // within a 1-second window. The Rust core fires one disconnect per-substream,
315 |     // producing 254+ events in under 1 second.
316 |     private var peerDisconnectDedupCache: [String: Date] = [:]
317 |     private let peerDisconnectDedupInterval: TimeInterval = 1.0
318 | 
319 |     // P4: Dedup cache — suppress redundant dial_throttled diagnostic log lines
320 |     // for the same address within a 5-minute window (9.6K+ events in a session).
321 |     private var dialThrottleLogCache: [String: Date] = [:]
322 |     private let dialThrottleLogInterval: TimeInterval = 300
323 | 
324 |     // MARK: - Published State
325 | 
326 |     var serviceState: ServiceState = .stopped
327 |     var serviceStats: ServiceStats?
328 |     var networkStatus = NetworkStatus()
329 |     private var discoveredPeerMap: [String: PeerDiscoveryInfo] = [:]
330 | 
331 |     struct NetworkStatus {
332 |         var wifi: Bool = false
333 |         var cellular: Bool = false
334 |         var available: Bool { wifi || cellular }
335 |     }
336 | 
337 |     // BLE Privacy Settings
338 |     var blePrivacyEnabled: Bool {
339 |         get { UserDefaults.standard.object(forKey: "ble_rotation_enabled") as? Bool ?? true }
340 |         set {
341 |             UserDefaults.standard.set(newValue, forKey: "ble_rotation_enabled")
342 |             blePeripheralManager?.setRotationEnabled(newValue)
343 |         }
344 |     }
345 | 
346 |     var blePrivacyInterval: TimeInterval {
347 |         get { UserDefaults.standard.object(forKey: "ble_rotation_interval") as? Double ?? 900 }
348 |         set {
349 |             UserDefaults.standard.set(newValue, forKey: "ble_rotation_interval")
350 |             blePeripheralManager?.setRotationInterval(newValue)
351 |         }
352 |     }
353 | 
354 |     var isAutoAdjustEnabled: Bool {
355 |         UserDefaults.standard.object(forKey: "auto_adjust_enabled") as? Bool ?? true
356 |     }
357 | 
358 |     // MARK: - Event Streams
359 | 
360 |     /// Stream of ALL message updates (sent and received)
361 |     let messageUpdates = PassthroughSubject<MessageRecord, Never>()
362 | 
363 |     /// Legacy alias for backward compatibility (filtered to received only if needed, but for now direct alias)
364 |     var incomingMessages: PassthroughSubject<MessageRecord, Never> { messageUpdates }
365 | 
366 |     let peerEvents = PassthroughSubject<PeerEvent, Never>()
367 |     let statusEvents = PassthroughSubject<StatusEvent, Never>()
368 | 
369 |     enum PeerEvent {
370 |         case discovered(peerId: String)
371 |         case connected(peerId: String)
372 |         case disconnected(peerId: String)
373 |     }
374 | 
375 |     enum StatusEvent {
376 |         case serviceStateChanged(ServiceState)
377 |         case statsUpdated(ServiceStats)
378 |     }
379 | 
380 |     // MARK: - Initialization
381 | 
382 |     init() {
383 |         // Use Application Support for internal app state (not user-facing docs).
384 |         let appSupportPath = FileManager.default.urls(for: .applicationSupportDirectory, in: .userDomainMask)[0]
385 |         let meshPath = appSupportPath.appendingPathComponent("mesh", isDirectory: true)
386 |         self.storagePath = meshPath.path
387 | 
388 |         // Load existing logs into memory buffer if they exist
389 |         if let existingLogs = try? String(contentsOf: diagnosticsLogURL, encoding: .utf8) {
390 |             let lines = existingLogs.components(separatedBy: .newlines).filter { !$0.isEmpty }
391 |             self.diagnosticsBuffer = Array(lines.suffix(diagnosticsMaxLines))
392 |         }
393 | 
394 |         logVerbose("MeshRepository initialized with storage: \(self.storagePath)")
395 |         if strictBleOnlyValidation {
396 |             logger.warning("Strict BLE-only validation mode is enabled (SC_BLE_ONLY_VALIDATION)")
397 |         }
398 | 
399 |         // Do NOT exclude the entire mesh directory from backup.
400 |         // history.db and contacts.db must survive app reinstalls via iCloud/device backup.
401 |         // The raw identity key directory (identity/) is excluded individually after start()
402 |         // since private keys are already protected in iOS Keychain.
403 | 
404 |         reconcileInstallScopedIdentityState()
405 | 
406 |         logDiagnostic("repo_init storage=\(self.storagePath)")
407 |         startHeartbeat()
408 |     }
409 | 
410 |     private func startHeartbeat() {
411 |         heartbeatTimer?.invalidate()
412 |         heartbeatTimer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 30, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
413 |             Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
414 |                 self?.logDiagnostic("pulse uptime=\(Int(Date().timeIntervalSinceReferenceDate) % 100000)")
415 |             }
416 |         }
417 |     }
418 | 
419 |     /// Initialize all managers
420 |     @MainActor
421 |     func initialize() throws {
422 |         logVerbose("Initializing managers")
423 |         logDiagnostic("repo_managers_init_start")
424 | 
425 |         do {
426 |             // Initialize data managers
427 |             settingsManager = MeshSettingsManager(storagePath: storagePath)
428 | 
429 |             // Ensure settings exist (load or create defaults)
430 |             if (try? settingsManager?.load()) == nil {
431 |                 logger.info("No settings found, applying defaults")
432 |                 if let defaults = settingsManager?.defaultSettings() {
433 |                     try? settingsManager?.save(settings: defaults)
434 |                 }
435 |             }
436 |             historyManager = try HistoryManager(storagePath: storagePath)
437 |             // WS12.41: Removed fixed 10k limit. Retention is now disk-percent aware.
438 |             // _ = try? historyManager?.enforceRetention(maxMessages: 10000)
439 |             contactManager = try ContactManager(storagePath: storagePath)
440 |             ledgerManager = LedgerManager(storagePath: storagePath)
441 |             autoAdjustEngine = AutoAdjustEngine()
442 | 
443 |             // Initialize transport managers
444 |             bleCentralManager = BLECentralManager(meshRepository: self)
445 |             blePeripheralManager = BLEPeripheralManager(meshRepository: self)
446 | 
447 |             // Pre-load data where applicable
448 |             try? ledgerManager?.load()
449 | 
450 |             logDiagnostic("repo_managers_init_success")
451 |             logVerbose("✓ All managers initialized successfully")
452 | 
453 |             // One-time migration: clear stale routing hints inherited from
454 |             // pre-fix builds that accumulated duplicate BLE MACs and stale
455 |             // libp2p_peer_id entries causing endless retry loops.
456 |             migrateStaleRoutingHints()
457 |         } catch {
458 |             logger.error("Failed to initialize managers: \(error.localizedDescription)")
459 |             throw error
460 |         }
461 |     }
462 | 
463 |     private func migrateStaleRoutingHints() {
464 |         let key = "v1_routing_hint_cleanup"
465 |         guard !UserDefaults.standard.bool(forKey: key) else { return }
466 | 
467 |         do {
468 |             let contacts = try contactManager?.list() ?? []
469 |             var cleaned = 0
470 |             for contact in contacts {
471 |                 guard let notes = contact.notes, !notes.isEmpty else { continue }
472 |                 guard notes.contains("libp2p_peer_id:") || notes.contains("ble_peer_id:") else { continue }
473 |                 // Strip stale routing entries — fresh discovery will repopulate them.
474 |                 let stripped = notes
475 |                     .split(whereSeparator: { $0 == ";" || $0 == "\n" })
476 |                     .map { String($0).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) }
477 |                     .filter { segment in
478 |                         !segment.hasPrefix("libp2p_peer_id:") &&
479 |                             !segment.hasPrefix("ble_peer_id:")
480 |                     }
481 |                     .joined(separator: ";")
482 |                 let updatedNotes: String? = stripped.isEmpty ? nil : stripped
483 |                 if updatedNotes != notes {
484 |                     var updated = contact
485 |                     updated.notes = updatedNotes
486 |                     try? contactManager?.add(contact: updated)
487 |                     cleaned += 1
488 |                 }
489 |             }
490 |             UserDefaults.standard.set(true, forKey: key)
491 |             if cleaned > 0 {
492 |                 logVerbose("Routing hint migration: cleaned \(cleaned) contact(s) with stale routing entries")
493 |             }
494 |         } catch {
495 |             logger.warning("Routing hint migration failed (non-fatal): \(error.localizedDescription)")
496 |         }
497 |     }
498 | 
499 |     /// Public start method called from App entry point

 941 |     // MARK: - Messaging (with Relay Enforcement)
 942 | 
 943 |     /// Send a message to a peer
 944 |     /// CRITICAL: Enforces relay = messaging coupling
 945 |     func sendMessage(peerId: String, content: String) async throws {
 946 |         logVerbose("Send message to \(peerId)")
 947 | 
 948 |         // RELAY ENFORCEMENT
 949 |         // Check if relay/messaging is enabled (bidirectional control)
 950 |         // Default to ENABLED when settings unavailable (matches Rust default: relay_enabled=true)
 951 |         let currentSettings = try? settingsManager?.load()
 952 |         let isRelayEnabled = currentSettings?.relayEnabled ?? true
 953 | 
 954 |         if !isRelayEnabled {
 955 |             let errorMsg = "Cannot send message: Relay is disabled. Enable relay in Settings to send and receive messages."
 956 |             logger.error("\(errorMsg)")
 957 |             throw MeshError.relayDisabled(errorMsg)
 958 |         }
 959 | 
 960 |         // Throttling: prevent sending identical content to same peer within 1s
 961 |         let throttleKey = "\(peerId):\(content.hashValue)"
 962 |         if let lastSend = transmissionThrottleCache[throttleKey], Date().timeIntervalSince(lastSend) < transmissionThrottleInterval {
 963 |             logVerbose("Throttling duplicate send for \(peerId)")
 964 |             return
 965 |         }
 966 |         transmissionThrottleCache[throttleKey] = Date()
 967 | 
 968 |         // Proceed with sending message
 969 |         guard let ironCore = ironCore else {
 970 |             throw MeshError.notInitialized("IronCore not initialized")
 971 |         }
 972 | 
 973 |         // Get recipient's public key
 974 |         let contact = try? contactManager?.get(peerId: peerId)
 975 |         guard let recipientPublicKey = contact?.publicKey else {
 976 |             throw MeshError.contactNotFound("Contact \(peerId) not found or has no public key")
 977 |         }
 978 | 
 979 |         // Pre-validate public key format to provide descriptive errors
 980 |         let trimmedKey = recipientPublicKey.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)

1087 |     func onMessageReceived(
1088 |         senderId: String,

1134 |             .nilIfEmpty

1449 |     func onDeliveryReceipt(messageId: String, status: String) {

1451 |         let normalizedMessageId = messageId.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
1452 |         guard normalized == "delivered" || normalized == "read" else { return }

1459 |             } else {
1460 |                 logDiagnostic("delivery_state msg=\(normalizedMessageId) state=pending detail=delivery_receipt_ignored_non_outbound status=\(normalized) direction=missing")
1461 |             }

1463 |         }

1468 |             } else {

Content: //
//  SmartTransportRouter.swift
//  SCMessenger
//
//  Smart transport selection with 500ms timeout fallback and transport health tracking.
//  Implements parallel transport racing for optimal message delivery latency.
//

import Foundation
import os

/// Transport types available for message delivery
enum TransportType: String, CaseIterable, Codable {
    case multipeer = "multipeer"
    case ble = "ble"
    case core = "core"  // libp2p/internet relay
}

/// Result of a transport delivery attempt
struct TransportDeliveryResult {
    let transport: TransportType
    let success: Bool
    let latencyMs: UInt64
    let error: String?
    let timestamp: Date
}

/// Health metrics for a specific transport to a specific peer
struct TransportHealth: Codable {
    var lastSuccessAt: Date?
    var lastFailureAt: Date?
    var successCount: UInt64 = 0
    var failureCount: UInt64 = 0
    var averageLatencyMs: Double = 0
    var lastLatencyMs: UInt64 = 0
    
    /// Success rate (0.0 to 1.0)
    var successRate: Double {
        let total = successCount + failureCount
        guard total > 0 else { return 0.5 } // Unknown = neutral
        return Double(successCount) / Double(total)
    }
    
    /// Is this transport considered healthy?
    var isHealthy: Bool {
        // Healthy if success rate > 50% and not too many recent failures
        guard successRate > 0.5 else { return false }
        // If last failure was very recent (< 5s), be cautious
        if let lastFailure = lastFailureAt {
            return Date().timeIntervalSince(lastFailure) > 5.0
        }
        return true
    }
    
    /// Score for transport selection (higher = better)
    var score: Double {
        // Weight: 70% success rate, 30% latency (inverted, lower latency = higher score)
        let latencyScore = averageLatencyMs > 0 ? min(1.0, 500.0 / averageLatencyMs) : 0.5
        return (successRate * 0.7) + (latencyScore * 0.3)
    }
}

/// Message deduplication entry
struct MessageDedupEntry {
    let messageId: String
    let firstReceivedAt: Date
    let firstTransport: TransportType
    var duplicateCount: UInt32 = 0
    var duplicateTimestamps: [Date] = []
    var duplicateTransports: [TransportType] = []
}

/// Smart transport router with health tracking and parallel racing
@MainActor
final class SmartTransportRouter {
    private let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.scmessenger", category: "TransportRouter")
    
    // Transport health tracking per peer
    private var transportHealth: [String: [TransportType: TransportHealth]] = [:]
    
    // Message deduplication cache
    private var messageDedupCache: [String: MessageDedupEntry] = [:]
    private let dedupCacheTtl: TimeInterval = 300 // 5 minutes
    
    // Timeout for "preferred" transport before racing all
    private let preferredTransportTimeoutMs: UInt64 = 500
    
    // Last successful transport per peer (for "previously used/good path")
    private var lastSuccessfulTransport: [String: TransportType] = [:]
    
    // MARK: - Transport Health Management
    
    /// Record a successful delivery
    func recordSuccess(peerId: String, transport: TransportType, latencyMs: UInt64) {
        var health = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport)
        health.lastSuccessAt = Date()
        health.successCount += 1
        health.lastLatencyMs = latencyMs
        
        // Update rolling average latency
        let totalDeliveries = health.successCount + health.failureCount
        if totalDeliveries > 1 {
            health.averageLatencyMs = ((health.averageLatencyMs * Double(totalDeliveries - 1)) + Double(latencyMs)) / Double(totalDeliveries)
        } else {
            health.averageLatencyMs = Double(latencyMs)
        }
        
        setHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport, health: health)
        lastSuccessfulTransport[peerId] = transport
        
        logger.info("Transport health updated: peer=\(peerId.prefix(8)) transport=\(transport.rawValue) success rate=\(String(format: "%.2f", health.successRate)) avg_latency=\(String(format: "%.0f", health.averageLatencyMs))ms")
    }
    
    /// Record a failed delivery
    func recordFailure(peerId: String, transport: TransportType, error: String?) {
        var health = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport)
        health.lastFailureAt = Date()
        health.failureCount += 1
        
        setHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport, health: health)
        
        logger.warning("Transport failure: peer=\(peerId.prefix(8)) transport=\(transport.rawValue) error=\(error ?? "unknown") success rate=\(String(format: "%.2f", health.successRate))")
    }
    
    /// Get health for a specific peer and transport
    private func getHealth(peerId: String, transport: TransportType) -> TransportHealth {
        return transportHealth[peerId]?[transport] ?? TransportHealth()
    }
    
    /// Set health for a specific peer and transport
    private func setHealth(peerId: String, transport: TransportType, health: TransportHealth) {
        if transportHealth[peerId] == nil {
            transportHealth[peerId] = [:]
        }
        transportHealth[peerId]?[transport] = health
    }
    
    // MARK: - Transport Selection
    
    /// Get the preferred transport for a peer (previously successful or highest score)
    func getPreferredTransport(peerId: String) -> TransportType? {
        // First check if we have a last successful transport
        if let lastSuccess = lastSuccessfulTransport[peerId] {
            let health = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: lastSuccess)
            if health.isHealthy {
                return lastSuccess
            }
        }
        
        // Otherwise, find the transport with the highest score
        let allTransports = TransportType.allCases
        var bestTransport: TransportType?
        var bestScore: Double = -1
        
        for transport in allTransports {
            let health = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport)
            if health.score > bestScore {
                bestScore = health.score
                bestTransport = transport
            }
        }
        
        return bestTransport
    }
    
    /// Get all available transports sorted by score (best first)
    func getAvailableTransportsSorted(peerId: String) -> [TransportType] {
        return TransportType.allCases.sorted { transport1, transport2 in
            let health1 = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport1)
            let health2 = getHealth(peerId: peerId, transport: transport2)
            return health1.score > health2.score
        }
    }
    
    // MARK: - Message Deduplication
    
    /// Check if a message is a duplicate and record it
    /// Returns: (isDuplicate, timeVarianceMs, firstTransport)
    func checkAndRecordMessage(
        messageId: String,
        transport: TransportType
    ) -> (isDuplicate: Bool, timeVarianceMs: UInt64?, firstTransport: TransportType?) {
        let now = Date()
        
        // Clean up old entries
        cleanupDedupCache()
        
        if let existing = messageDedupCache[messageId] {
            // This is a duplicate
            let timeVarianceMs = UInt64(now.timeIntervalSince(existing.firstReceivedAt) * 1000)
            
            var updated = existing
            updated.duplicateCount += 1
            updated.duplicateTimestamps.append(now)
            updated.duplicateTransports.append(transport)
            messageDedupCache[messageId] = updated
            
            logger.info("Message duplicate detected: msg=\(messageId.prefix(8)) transport=\(transport.rawValue) time_variance=\(timeVarianceMs)ms first_transport=\(existing.firstTransport.rawValue) duplicate_count=\(updated.duplicateCount)")
            
            return (isDuplicate: true, timeVarianceMs: timeVarianceMs, firstTransport: existing.firstTransport)
        } else {
            // First receipt of this message
            let entry = MessageDedupEntry(
                messageId: messageId,
                firstReceivedAt: now,
                firstTransport: transport,
                duplicateCount: 0,
                duplicateTimestamps: [],
                duplicateTransports: []
            )
            messageDedupCache[messageId] = entry
            
            logger.info("Message first receipt: msg=\(messageId.prefix(8)) transport=\(transport.rawValue)")
            
            return (isDuplicate: false, timeVarianceMs: nil, firstTransport: nil)
        }
    }
    
    /// Get dedup statistics for a message (for mesh enhancement logging)
    func getDedupStats(messageId: String) -> MessageDedupEntry? {
        return messageDedupCache[messageId]
    }
    
    /// Clean up old dedup cache entries
    private func cleanupDedupCache() {
        let cutoff = Date().addingTimeInterval(-dedupCacheTtl)
        messageDedupCache = messageDedupCache.filter { _, entry in
            entry.firstReceivedAt > cutoff
        }
    }
    
    // MARK: - Smart Delivery
    
    /// Attempt delivery with smart transport selection
    /// - Tries preferred transport first
    /// - If no response within 500ms, races all available transports
    /// - Returns the first successful result
    func attemptDelivery(
        peerId: String,
        envelopeData: Data,
        multipeerPeerId: String?,
        blePeerId: String?,
        routePeerCandidates: [String],
        addresses: [String],
        traceMessageId: String?,
        attemptContext: String?,
        tryMultipeer: @escaping (String) async -> Bool,
        tryBle: @escaping (String) async -> Bool,
        tryCore: @escaping (String) async -> Bool
    ) async -> TransportDeliveryResult {
        let startTime = Date()
        
        // Determine available transports
        var availableTransports: [(type: TransportType, target: String, attempt: () async -> Bool)] = []
        
        if let multipeerTarget = multipeerPeerId?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines), !multipeerTarget.isEmpty {
            availableTransports.append((.multipeer, multipeerTarget, { await tryMultipeer(multipeerTarget) }))
        }
        
        if let bleTarget = blePeerId?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines), !bleTarget.isEmpty {
            availableTransports.append((.ble, bleTarget, { await tryBle(bleTarget) }))
        }
        
        if let coreTarget = routePeerCandidates.first?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines), !coreTarget.isEmpty {
            availableTransports.append((.core, coreTarget, { await tryCore(coreTarget) }))
        }
        
        guard !availableTransports.isEmpty else {
            logger.warning("No available transports for peer \(peerId.prefix(8))")
            return TransportDeliveryResult(
                transport: .core,
                success: false,
                latencyMs: 0,
                error: "no_available_transports",
                timestamp: Date()
            )
        }
        
        // Get preferred transport
        let preferredTransport = getPreferredTransport(peerId: peerId)
        
        // If we have a preferred transport, try it first with timeout
        if let preferred = preferredTransport,
           let preferredAttempt = availableTransports.first(where: { $0.type == preferred }) {
            logger.info("Trying preferred transport \(preferred.rawValue) for peer \(peerId.prefix(8))")
            
            // Race preferred transport against timeout
            let preferredResult = await withTaskGroup(of: Bool?.self, returning: Bool?.self) { group in
                // Preferred transport attempt
                group.addTask {
                    await preferredAttempt.attempt()
                }
                
                // Timeout task
                group.addTask {
                    try? await Task.sleep(nanoseconds: self.preferredTransportTimeoutMs * 1_000_000)
                    return nil // Timeout signal
                }
                
                // Wait for first result
                let result = await group.next()
                group.cancelAll()
                return result ?? nil
            }
            
            if let success = preferredResult, success {
                let latencyMs = UInt64(Date().timeIntervalSince(startTime) * 1000)
                recordSuccess(peerId: peerId, transport: preferred, latencyMs: latencyMs)
                logger.info("✓ Preferred transport \(preferred.rawValue) succeeded in \(latencyMs)ms")
                return TransportDeliveryResult(
                    transport: preferred,
                    success: true,
                    latencyMs: latencyMs,
                    error: nil,
                    timestamp: Date()
                )
            }
            
            // Preferred transport failed or timed out - race all transports
            logger.warning("Preferred transport \(preferred.rawValue) failed/timed out, racing all transports")
        }
        
        // Race all available transports in parallel
        logger.info("Racing \(availableTransports.count) transports for peer \(peerId.prefix(8))")
        
        let result = await withTaskGroup(
            of: (transport: TransportType, success: Bool, latencyMs: UInt64)?.self,
            returning: (transport: TransportType, success: Bool, latencyMs: UInt64)?.self
        ) { group in
            for transportAttempt in availableTransports {
                group.addTask {
                    let transportStart = Date()
                    let success = await transportAttempt.attempt()
                    let latencyMs = UInt64(Date().timeIntervalSince(transportStart) * 1000)
                    return (transport: transportAttempt.type, success: success, latencyMs: latencyMs)
                }
            }
            
            // Return first successful result
            for await result in group {
                if let result = result, result.success {
                    group.cancelAll()
                    return result
                }
            }
            
            return nil
        }
        
        if let result = result {
            recordSuccess(peerId: peerId, transport: result.transport, latencyMs: result.latencyMs)
            logger.info("✓ Transport \(result.transport.rawValue) succeeded in \(result.latencyMs)ms")
            return TransportDeliveryResult(
                transport: result.transport,
                success: true,
                latencyMs: result.latencyMs,
                error: nil,
                timestamp: Date()
            )
        } else {
            // All transports failed
            let latencyMs = UInt64(Date().timeIntervalSince(startTime) * 1000)
            for transportAttempt in availableTransports {
                recordFailure(peerId: peerId, transport: transportAttempt.type, error: "all_transports_failed")
            }
            logger.error("✗ All transports failed for peer \(peerId.prefix(8))")
            return TransportDeliveryResult(
                transport: .core,
                success: false,
                latencyMs: latencyMs,
                error: "all_transports_failed",
                timestamp: Date()
            )
        }
    }
    
    // MARK: - Diagnostics
    
    /// Get transport health summary for diagnostics
    func getHealthSummary() -> [String: [String: Any]] {
        var summary: [String: [String: Any]] = [:]
        
        for (peerId, transports) in transportHealth {
            var peerSummary: [String: Any] = [:]
            for (transport, health) in transports {
                peerSummary[transport.rawValue] = [
                    "success_rate": health.successRate,
                    "avg_latency_ms": health.averageLatencyMs,
                    "success_count": health.successCount,
                    "failure_count": health.failureCount,
                    "is_healthy": health.isHealthy,
                    "score": health.score
                ]
            }
            summary[peerId] = peerSummary
        }
        
        return summary
    }
    
    /// Reset health for a peer (e.g., after reconnection)
    func resetHealth(peerId: String) {
        transportHealth.removeValue(forKey: peerId)
        lastSuccessfulTransport.removeValue(forKey: peerId)
        logger.info("Transport health reset for peer \(peerId.prefix(8))")
    }
}

                        return false
                    }
                    do {
                        try transport.sendData(toPeerId: multipeerAddr, data: envelopeData)
                        logger.info("✓ Delivery via Multipeer (target=\(multipeerAddr))")
                        logDeliveryAttempt(
                            messageId: traceMessageId,
                            medium: "multipeer",
                            phase: "local_fallback",
                            outcome: "success",
                            detail: "ctx=\(attemptContext) target=\(multipeerAddr)"
                        )
                        return true
                    } catch {
                        logger.debug("tryMultipeerDelivery failed for \(multipeerAddr): \(error.localizedDescription)")
                        logDeliveryAttempt(
                            messageId: traceMessageId,
                            medium: "multipeer",
                            phase: "local_fallback",
                            outcome: "failed",
                            detail: "ctx=\(attemptContext) target=\(multipeerAddr) reason=\(error.localizedDescription)"
                        )
                        return false
                    }
                },
                tryBle: { bleAddr in
                    logger.debug("tryBleDelivery: given blePeerId=\(bleAddr)")
                    let sendTargets = ([bleAddr] + connectedBlePeerIds)
                        .map { $0.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) }
                        .filter { !$0.isEmpty }
                        .reduce(into: [String]()) { deduped, next in
                            if !deduped.contains(next) {
                                deduped.append(next)
                            }
                        }
                    if sendTargets.isEmpty {
                        logger.debug("tryBleDelivery: no send targets available")
                        return false
                    }

                    var lastFailureReason = "no_target_attempted"
                    for target in sendTargets {
                        // Prefer Peripheral path first (notifications to subscribed Android central)
                        // This is the ONLY reliable iOS→Android path when WiFi is off
                        if let peripheral = blePeripheralManager {
                            if peripheral.sendDataToConnectedCentral(peerId: target, data: envelopeData) {
                                logDeliveryAttempt(
                                    messageId: traceMessageId,
                                    medium: "ble",
                                    phase: "local_fallback",
                                    outcome: "accepted",
                                    detail: "ctx=\(attemptContext) role=peripheral requested_target=\(bleAddr) target=\(target)"
                                )
                                return true
                            }
                            lastFailureReason = "peripheral_send_false:\(target)"
                        }

                        // Fallback: Central path (write to Android's GATT server)
                        if let central = bleCentralManager {
                            if let uuid = UUID(uuidString: target) {
                                if central.sendData(to: uuid, data: envelopeData) {
                                    logger.info("✓ Delivery via BLE Central (target=\(target))")
                                    logDeliveryAttempt(
                                        messageId: traceMessageId,
                                        medium: "ble",
                                        phase: "local_fallback",
                                        outcome: "accepted",
                                        detail: "ctx=\(attemptContext) role=central requested_target=\(bleAddr) target=\(target)"
                                    )
                                    return true
                                }
                                lastFailureReason = "central_send_false:\(target)"
                            } else {
                                lastFailureReason = "central_invalid_uuid:\(target)"
                            }
                        }
                    }

                    logDeliveryAttempt(
                        messageId: traceMessageId,
                        medium: "ble",
                        phase: "local_fallback",
                        outcome: "failed",
                        detail: "ctx=\(attemptContext) requested_target=\(bleAddr) reason=\(lastFailureReason) connected=\(connectedBlePeerIds.count)"
                    )
                    return false
                }
            )
            smartResult = TransportDeliveryResult(
                transport: localFallback.acked ? (localFallback.multipeerAcked ? .multipeer : .ble) : .core,
                success: localFallback.acked,
                latencyMs: 0,
                error: localFallback.acked ? nil : "legacy_fallback_failed",
                timestamp: Date()
            )
        }
        
        let localAcked = smartResult.success

        // Process message
        let content = decodedPayload.text

        // Smart deduplication: check if this is a duplicate and track time variance
        let dedupResult = smartTransportRouter?.checkAndRecordMessage(
            messageId: messageId,
            transport: .core // Default transport for incoming messages
        )
        
        if let existing = try? historyManager?.get(id: messageId),
           existing.direction == .received {
            logVerbose("Duplicate inbound message \(messageId); acknowledging without UI emit")
            
            // Log duplicate with time variance for mesh enhancement
            if let dedup = dedupResult, dedup.isDuplicate, let timeVariance = dedup.timeVarianceMs {
                logDiagnostic("msg_duplicate msg=\(messageId) time_variance_ms=\(timeVariance) first_transport=\(dedup.firstTransport?.rawValue ?? "unknown") duplicate_count=\(smartTransportRouter?.getDedupStats(messageId: messageId)?.duplicateCount ?? 0)")
            }
            
            sendDeliveryReceiptAsync(
                senderPublicKeyHex: senderPublicKeyHex,
                messageId: messageId,
                senderId: canonicalPeerId,
                preferredRoutePeerId: routePeerId,
                preferredListenerHints: hintedDialCandidates
            )
            return
        }
        
        // First receipt of this message - log for mesh enhancement tracking
        if let dedup = dedupResult, !dedup.isDuplicate {
            logDiagnostic("msg_first_receipt msg=\(messageId) transport=\(dedup.firstTransport?.rawValue ?? "unknown")")

 22 | /**
 23 |  * Repository abstracting access to the Rust core via UniFFI bindings.
 24 |  *
 25 |  * This is the single source of truth for:
 26 |  * - Mesh service lifecycle
 27 |  * - Contacts management
 28 |  * - Message history
 29 |  * - Connection ledger
 30 |  * - Network settings
 31 |  *
 32 |  * All UniFFI objects are initialized lazily and managed here to ensure
 33 |  * proper lifecycle and resource cleanup.
 34 |  */
 35 | open class MeshRepository(private val context: Context) {
 36 | 
 37 |     companion object {
 38 |         private const val IDENTITY_BACKUP_PREFS = "identity_backup_prefs"
 39 |         private const val IDENTITY_BACKUP_KEY = "identity_backup_v1"
 40 |         /** Static fallback bootstrap nodes for NAT traversal and internet roaming.
 41 |          *  These are used if env override and remote fetch both fail/are absent.
 42 |          *  Priority order: GCP relay (cloud) → OSX relay (home/local backup). */
 43 |         private val STATIC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES: List<String> = listOf(
 44 |             // GCP relay — primary cloud node

 46 |             // OSX home relay — secondary backup (this machine, port 9010)

 48 |         )
 49 | 
 50 |         /** Resolve bootstrap nodes using the core BootstrapResolver.
 51 |          *  Priority: SC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES env var → remote URL → static fallback. */
 52 |         val DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_NODES: List<String> by lazy {
 53 |             try {
 54 |                 val config = uniffi.api.BootstrapConfig(
 55 |                     staticNodes = STATIC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES,
 56 |                     remoteUrl = "https://bootstrap.scmessenger.net/nodes.json",
 57 |                     fetchTimeoutSecs = 5u,

 59 |                 )

 61 |             } catch (e: Exception) {

 63 |                 STATIC_BOOTSTRAP_NODES
 64 |             }
 65 |         }

 68 |             // Default to ENABLED when settings unavailable (matches Rust default: relay_enabled=true)
 69 |             return settings?.relayEnabled ?: true
 70 |         }
 71 |

 73 |             if (!isMeshParticipationEnabled(settings)) {
 74 |                 throw IllegalStateException(
 75 |                     "Cannot send messages: mesh participation is disabled. Enable mesh participation in settings to send and receive messages."
 76 |                 )
 77 |             }
 78 |         }

 81 |             return when (raw?.trim()?.lowercase()) {
 82 |                 "1", "true", "yes", "on" -> true
 83 |                 else -> false
 84 |             }
 85 |         }

 88 |             val wifiAttempted: Boolean,
 89 |             val wifiAcked: Boolean,
 90 |             val bleAttempted: Boolean,
 91 |             val bleAcked: Boolean
 92 |         ) {
 93 |             val acked: Boolean

 95 |         }

 98 |             wifiPeerId: String?,
 99 |             blePeerId: String?,
100 |             tryWifi: (String) -> Boolean,
101 |             tryBle: (String) -> Boolean
102 |         ): LocalTransportFallbackResult {
103 |             val normalizedWifi = wifiPeerId?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
104 |             if (normalizedWifi != null) {
105 |                 if (tryWifi(normalizedWifi)) {
106 |                     return LocalTransportFallbackResult(
107 |                         wifiAttempted = true,
108 |                         wifiAcked = true,
109 |                         bleAttempted = false,
110 |                         bleAcked = false
111 |                     )
112 |                 }
113 |             }
114 | 
115 |             val normalizedBle = blePeerId?.trim()?.takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
116 |             if (normalizedBle != null) {
117 |                 val bleAcked = tryBle(normalizedBle)
118 |                 return LocalTransportFallbackResult(
119 |                     wifiAttempted = normalizedWifi != null,
120 |                     wifiAcked = false,
121 |                     bleAttempted = true,
122 |                     bleAcked = bleAcked
123 |                 )
124 |             }
125 | 
126 |             return LocalTransportFallbackResult(
127 |                 wifiAttempted = normalizedWifi != null,
128 |                 wifiAcked = false,
129 |                 bleAttempted = false,
130 |                 bleAcked = false
131 |             )
132 |         }
133 |     }
